steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure on each side of the blank
steps.content: Left side: A noun phrase serving as the subject (A 2024 study by archaeologist Guadalupe Romero Villanueva and colleagues). Right side: A dependent clause (“that comb-shaped motifs are approximately 8,200 years old”) immediately followed by an independent clause with the verb offers.

steps.title: Apply the participle rule to prevent run-on sentences
steps.content: When adding information about the subject without creating a second main verb, use a participle to form a modifying phrase; reporting creates such a phrase, leaving offers as the sentence's sole main verb. Finite verbs in the blank (reported, has reported, reports) would create two main clauses without punctuation, producing a run-on sentence. That matches choice D — D is correct.
